text.title: Headroom Cost Model Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Headroom Cost Model methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Model MUST inventory every critical resource: compute (per service), db (per instance), network (NAT + LB + transit), storage (per bucket / volume class). Skipped resources flagged.
rule.rationale: Missing resources surprise capacity planning during a scale event.

rule.statement: Utilisation measured as p95 over trailing 30 days, NOT instantaneous. Spike-driven over-provisioning rejected.
rule.rationale: Instantaneous spikes inflate cost; p95-30d balances safety + cost.

rule.statement: Headroom thresholds: 70% triggers a re-provision ticket; 90% triggers an immediate re-provision action + on-call notification.
rule.rationale: 70% is enough lead time to plan; 90% is the last responsible moment before saturation.

rule.statement: Each resource MUST have a cost-per-unit recorded (per vCPU-hour, per GB-month, per request). Cost projection multiplies projected unit demand by unit cost.
rule.rationale: Without unit cost, the bill is a mystery; with it, optimisations target the right resource.

rule.statement: Quarterly projection MUST forecast next-quarter resource demand (driven by product roadmap traffic forecast) + cost. Projection delta vs current shared with finance + engineering leader.
rule.rationale: Quarterly cadence aligns with finance + product cycles; surprises avoided.

rule.statement: Cost anomalies (>30% deviation from projection) MUST trigger an investigation within 1 business day. Anomaly source documented in the model.
rule.rationale: 30% deviation is the threshold where projection becomes invalid; 1-day SLA prevents bill snowball.
